catalogue_policy.authority: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Revision 2.3

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: one aligned IA-64 instruction bundle
operation.encoding: 128 bits: template 4:0, slot 0 45:5, slot 1 86:46, slot 2 127:87

legal_inputs: A 16-byte-aligned 128-bit bundle containing a defined template and legal slot encodings

privilege.rule: Bundle layout is independent of privilege level

state_read: 128 instruction bits at the aligned bundle address

predication.rule: Predication affects decoded instructions, not bundle field extraction

rows.nat_behavior: Bundle field extraction does not read or write NaT state
rows.committed_prefix: No architectural instruction result is committed by bundle field extraction alone
rows.restart_rule: A fetch interruption is retried at the interrupted bundle and slot under the applicable interruption rules
permitted_results: Exactly one 5-bit template and three ordered 41-bit instruction slots are extracted from the specified bit ranges

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Template Field Encoding and Instruction Slot Mapping

operation.selector: defined template encodings from Table 3-10
operation.encoding: template values 0x00-0x1d with reserved values excluded

legal_inputs: 0x00 MII
0x01 MII stop2
0x02 MI stop1 I
0x03 MI stop1 I stop2
0x04 MLX
0x05 MLX stop2
0x08 MMI
0x09 MMI stop2
0x0a M stop0 MI
0x0b M stop0 MI stop2
0x0c MFI
0x0d MFI stop2
0x0e MMF
0x0f MMF stop2
0x10 MIB
0x11 MIB stop2
0x12 MBB
0x13 MBB stop2
0x16 BBB
0x17 BBB stop2
0x18 MMB
0x19 MMB stop2
0x1c MFB
0x1d MFB stop2

privilege.rule: Template unit and stop mappings are independent of privilege level

predication.rule: Predication does not change template unit or stop mappings

rows.nat_behavior: Template decoding does not read or write NaT state
rows.committed_prefix: Template decoding alone commits no instruction result
rows.restart_rule: A fetch interruption is retried at the interrupted bundle and slot under the applicable interruption rules
permitted_results: Each legal input maps to exactly the listed slot-unit sequence and stop positions

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: sequential bundles and slots
operation.encoding: bundle addresses increase first; within a bundle slots increase from 0 through 2

legal_inputs: A legal instruction stream spanning one or more adjacent bundles

privilege.rule: Instruction ordering is independent of privilege level

state_read: instruction stream in increasing bundle-address and slot order
state_written: architectural state written by the ordered instructions

predication.rule: Predication may dynamically disable an instruction's resource accesses without changing its position in instruction order

rows.nat_behavior: NaT behavior is determined by each ordered instruction
rows.committed_prefix: For an interruption, commitment follows the separately catalogued instruction-group rule
rows.restart_rule: Resume uses the architecturally saved bundle address and slot
permitted_results: Bundles are ordered by increasing memory address and instructions within each bundle are ordered slot 0, slot 1, slot 2

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Template Field Encoding and Instruction Slot Mapping

operation.selector: all 24 defined template encodings
operation.encoding: exact M/I/F/B/L/X slot types, internal stops, terminal stops, and MLX designation

legal_inputs: Each defined template with a legal no-effect instruction in every named unit

privilege.rule: Template interpretation is independent of privilege

state_read: five-bit template field and three instruction slots
state_written: state written by the decoded instructions

predication.rule: Template interpretation precedes instruction predication

rows.nat_behavior: Template interpretation does not itself consume or produce NaT
rows.committed_prefix: Stops partition issue groups exactly as encoded
rows.restart_rule: Resume retains the template interpretation at the saved bundle and RI
permitted_results: All defined templates produce exactly the manual's unit and stop mapping

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 3: Instruction Set Reference
specification.anchor: The X-unit instructions occupy two instruction slots

operation.selector: MOVL and BRL L+X formats
operation.encoding: one logical instruction combines the L-slot immediate extension with the X-slot opcode and immediate fields

legal_inputs: Legal positive and negative long immediates and bundle-aligned branch targets

privilege.rule: Application L+X instructions execute at every privilege level

state_read: both L and X slots and current bundle IP for BRL
state_written: MOVL destination GR or BRL target IP

predication.rule: The X-slot qualifying predicate governs the logical L+X instruction

rows.nat_behavior: MOVL clears the destination NaT; BRL does not consume NaT
rows.committed_prefix: The L and X slots commit as one logical instruction
rows.restart_rule: A legal L+X instruction restarts at its logical slot 1
permitted_results: Exact 64-bit MOVL constant or signed 60-bit BRL target reconstruction

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture
specification.anchor: Attempts to execute a bundle of template MLX when PSR.ri == 2

operation.selector: reserved templates and MLX entry with RI equal to two
operation.encoding: eight reserved template values and an invalid L+X continuation entry

legal_inputs: Interruption collection enabled before the illegal bundle is entered

privilege.rule: Illegal bundle and slot placement is detected at every privilege level

state_read: template, saved RI, and instruction bits

predication.rule: Template and MLX continuation legality is checked independently of qp

rows.nat_behavior: An illegal placement does not change NaT state
fault_priority: Bundle/template placement is rejected before instruction effects
rows.committed_prefix: No instruction in the illegal bundle commits
rows.restart_rule: IIP names the target bundle and IPSR.ri/ISR.ei preserve RI 2 for invalid MLX continuation entry
permitted_results: Precise Illegal Operation with unchanged instruction destinations

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 3: Instruction Set Reference

operation.selector: ignored and reserved instruction fields
operation.encoding: nonzero ignored X3 L bits and nonzero reserved POPCNT r2

legal_inputs: Both enabled and false-qualified encodings with distinguishable destination and suffix markers

privilege.rule: Reserved and ignored field rules are independent of privilege

predication.rule: A false qualifying predicate suppresses reserved-field behavior where specified

rows.nat_behavior: Ignored fields do not change NaT; a reserved-field fault commits no target NaT
fault_priority: False qp nullifies the instruction; otherwise a reserved field faults before target or suffix effects
rows.committed_prefix: Only instructions before the reserved encoding may commit
rows.restart_rule: The saved bundle and RI identify the reserved instruction
permitted_results: Ignored fields do not affect the legal result; enabled reserved fields fault precisely

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture
specification.anchor: instruction bundle address contained in IIP, along with the IPSR.ri field

operation.selector: completion, control transfer, trap, external interruption, and RFI
operation.encoding: architectural IP plus PSR.ri or IIP plus IPSR.ri

legal_inputs: Sequential groups, a taken transfer, an enabled branch trap, and an enabled external interrupt

privilege.rule: The system-state probe executes at privilege level zero

state_read: IP, PSR.ri, branch target, interruption resources

predication.rule: Only an enabled taken transfer changes the sequential IP path

rows.nat_behavior: IP/RI transitions do not themselves read or write NaT
fault_priority: Completed branch trap state names the target; an external interruption saves the interrupted instruction before handler entry
rows.committed_prefix: Completed instructions precede the saved IP/RI state
rows.restart_rule: RFI restores the saved bundle and RI
permitted_results: Every transition exposes the exact architectural bundle and slot state

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture
specification.anchor: instruction bundle address contained in IIP, along with the IPSR.ri field

operation.selector: architectural RI values zero, one, and two
operation.encoding: one MII bundle with distinct slot destination markers

legal_inputs: A legal bundle entered at each valid architectural slot number

privilege.rule: RI selects an instruction slot independently of current privilege

state_read: IP, PSR.ri, and the selected bundle suffix
state_written: destinations of the selected slot and following slots plus completed IP/RI

predication.rule: The matrix uses p0 so RI selection is observed directly

rows.nat_behavior: The selected ADD destinations clear NaT; skipped slots leave destinations unchanged
rows.committed_prefix: Slots before the entry RI are not replayed
rows.restart_rule: Execution starts at exactly the saved RI and continues through the legal suffix
permitted_results: RI 0 executes slots 0-2, RI 1 executes slots 1-2, and RI 2 executes slot 2

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Bundles are ordered from lowest to highest memory address

operation.selector: instruction groups split by translator limits or a 4 KiB page boundary
operation.encoding: no-stop multi-bundle groups with group-entry dependencies

legal_inputs: Groups longer than one translation block and groups crossing from 0xff0 to 0x1000

privilege.rule: The direct-TCG probes execute at privilege level zero

state_read: ordered bundles and group-entry GR, NaT, and predicate state
state_written: the same architectural destinations as an unsplit group

predication.rule: A boundary may not alter predicate visibility

rows.nat_behavior: Saved group-entry GR values remain paired with their NaT bits across boundaries
rows.committed_prefix: A nonarchitectural translation boundary commits no extra prefix
rows.restart_rule: Translation segmentation does not change architectural restart state
permitted_results: Split and unsplit execution have identical ordered results and visibility

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: direct, indirect, long, conditional, counted, top, exit, and while-loop forms
operation.encoding: architecturally legal B-unit and MLX branch encodings

legal_inputs: Legal branch forms in their permitted slots with bundle-aligned targets

privilege.rule: Application branch forms execute at every privilege level subject to target privilege checks

state_read: IP, qualifying predicate or LC/EC, and branch register where applicable
state_written: IP and form-specific link, frame, loop, epilog, and rotating state

predication.rule: Conditional and while-loop forms use the qualifying predicate; counted loop forms do not

rows.nat_behavior: A NaT branch-register source is governed by the applicable branch-register transfer and fault rules
fault_priority: An illegal slot or encoding faults before transfer
rows.committed_prefix: A taken branch suppresses its suffix; a not-taken branch permits sequential execution
rows.restart_rule: Faults restart at the branch; traps report the completed transfer under interruption rules
permitted_results: Each legal form selects exactly its architecturally defined target or fallthrough and updates only form-specific state

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: taken if the specified predicate is 1

operation.selector: true, false, and p0 branch conditions
operation.encoding: legal conditional direct and indirect branch encodings

legal_inputs: True and false qualifying predicates with valid target and fallthrough bundles

privilege.rule: Outcome selection is independent of privilege before target checks

state_read: Qualifying predicate and branch target source
state_written: IP and any taken-form side effects

predication.rule: A one predicate takes the branch and a zero predicate falls through

rows.nat_behavior: Predicate registers do not carry NaT
rows.committed_prefix: Only instructions through the first taken branch in a group execute
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for valid mapped targets and fallthroughs
permitted_results: True and p0 select the target; false selects the sequential bundle

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: forward and backward IP-relative and long displacements
operation.encoding: signed 21-bit B-unit or signed 60-bit MLX displacement

legal_inputs: Positive, zero-equivalent fallthrough, and negative in-range displacements

privilege.rule: Displacement construction is independent of privilege

state_read: Bundle IP and encoded displacement fields

predication.rule: Target construction is observed when the branch condition is true

rows.nat_behavior: Immediate displacement construction does not consume NaT
fault_priority: Target-address checks apply after the branch condition is resolved
rows.committed_prefix: A taken branch commits the signed target and suppresses its suffix
rows.restart_rule: A target-address fault or trap follows branch interruption rules
permitted_results: The sign-extended displacement is added to the containing bundle IP without truncation

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: ordinary and same-group-forwarded branch-register targets
operation.encoding: legal indirect B-unit branch encodings

legal_inputs: Branch-register targets with aligned and nonzero low address bits

privilege.rule: Target privilege is checked after target selection

state_read: Selected branch register and qualifying predicate

predication.rule: A false predicate must not transfer to or validate the target

rows.nat_behavior: Branch registers do not carry NaT
fault_priority: A false predicate suppresses target-dependent effects
rows.committed_prefix: A taken indirect branch uses one selected branch-register image and suppresses its suffix
rows.restart_rule: A target interruption reports the branch under the architectural interruption rules
permitted_results: The selected target is bundle-aligned and transfer begins at slot 0

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: cloop, ctop, cexit, wtop, and wexit truth-table states
operation.encoding: legal slot-2 loop branch encodings

legal_inputs: Boundary and interior LC/EC values with true and false while-loop predicates

privilege.rule: Loop state transitions are independent of privilege

state_read: LC, EC, qualifying predicate where applicable, and CFM rotating bases
state_written: IP, LC, EC, rotating bases, and rotating predicate stage state

predication.rule: While-loop forms use a qualifying predicate; counted forms use LC and EC

rows.nat_behavior: Loop control registers and predicates do not carry NaT; rotating GR NaT bits rotate with their registers
fault_priority: A loop branch outside slot 2 faults before loop-state updates
rows.committed_prefix: The selected loop transition updates count, epilog, and rotation state exactly once
rows.restart_rule: An illegal loop encoding or slot restarts at the faulting branch without loop-state change
permitted_results: Direction and LC/EC/rotation updates match the complete architectural boundary truth table

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture

operation.selector: taken-branch and privilege-demoting transfer trap enables
operation.encoding: legal taken branch or return with PSR.tb or PSR.lp enabled

legal_inputs: Valid taken targets with taken-branch trapping or a lower target privilege enabled

privilege.rule: Privilege demotion with PSR.lp set raises the lower-privilege transfer trap

state_read: PSR.tb, PSR.lp, current and target privilege, branch condition and target
state_written: Interruption state including IIP, IIPA, IPSR, IFS, ISR, and vector transfer

predication.rule: Only a taken branch can raise these branch-completion traps

rows.nat_behavior: Trap enable and branch target state do not consume GR NaT for the tested return forms
fault_priority: Lower-privilege and taken-branch trap priority follows the interruption priority rules
rows.committed_prefix: The branch completes before its trap state is collected; its suffix remains suppressed
rows.restart_rule: RFI resumes from the trap handler's saved architectural continuation
permitted_results: The enabled trap vector and exact saved branch state are reported after a taken transfer

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: only instructions up to the taken branch will be executed

operation.selector: slot, page, helper, and translation-block execution boundaries
operation.encoding: legal direct, indirect, call, and return branch encodings

legal_inputs: Equivalent branch programs placed across each declared execution boundary

privilege.rule: Internal execution boundaries cannot alter architectural branch semantics

state_read: Branch inputs and complete pre-boundary architectural state

predication.rule: Qualification and first-taken suppression remain identical across all boundaries

rows.nat_behavior: Boundary splitting cannot change branch-related NaT behavior
rows.committed_prefix: Every boundary placement preserves the same prefix, taken suffix suppression, and fallthrough behavior
rows.restart_rule: Internal helper or TB boundaries are not guest-visible restart points
permitted_results: All boundary placements produce identical architectural branch state

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: producer and consumer separated by an architectural stop
operation.encoding: any legal templates that place the producer and consumer in adjacent instruction groups

legal_inputs: A legal producer followed by a stop and a legal consumer of its result

privilege.rule: The rule applies at every privilege level where both instructions are legal

state_read: architectural state at the start of the consumer's instruction group

predication.rule: Only dynamically enabled resource accesses participate

rows.nat_behavior: A NaT value committed by the producer is visible to the later group according to the consumer's NaT semantics
rows.committed_prefix: All updates from the previous instruction group precede reads in the next group
rows.restart_rule: Any instruction fault uses its separately catalogued restart rule
permitted_results: The consumer reads architectural state after all updates from the producer's instruction group

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: multiple legal register accesses in one instruction group
operation.encoding: legal dependency cases only; prohibited RAW and WAW cases are outside this row

legal_inputs: A well-formed instruction group satisfying the architectural dependency restrictions

privilege.rule: The rule applies at every privilege level where the instructions are legal

state_read: register state at instruction-group entry except for architecturally specified dependency special cases
state_written: register updates produced by the instruction group

predication.rule: Dependency restrictions apply only to resource reads and writes not dynamically disabled by predication

rows.nat_behavior: GR NaT bits are part of register state and follow the same group-entry rule unless a specified dependency exception applies
rows.committed_prefix: Instruction-group completion exposes the final architecturally permitted register updates
rows.restart_rule: Any instruction fault uses the separately catalogued instruction-group commitment and restart rules
permitted_results: Each register read observes group-entry register state rather than another instruction's update in the same group, except for specified dependency special cases

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Exceptions are reported in instruction order

operation.selector: a correctly formed instruction group with one or more instruction exceptions
operation.encoding: faulting instruction at any legal slot in a dependency-correct group

legal_inputs: A correctly formed instruction group satisfying resource dependency requirements and containing an architecturally defined exception

privilege.rule: The rule applies at every privilege level where the instructions and exception are applicable

state_read: ordered instructions and their exception conditions
state_written: architectural updates from instructions before the reported exception

predication.rule: Dynamically disabled resource accesses do not create the corresponding exception or dependency

rows.nat_behavior: NaT state written by earlier completed instructions is part of the committed prefix; later NaT updates are not committed
possible_faults: Any architecturally defined instruction exception applicable to the selected instruction
fault_priority: Report the exception on the earliest instruction when multiple instructions in the group raise exceptions
rows.committed_prefix: All prior instructions have updated architectural state and all subsequent instructions have not updated architectural state at exception delivery
rows.restart_rule: Repair and retry begins at the saved faulting instruction without replaying the committed prefix
permitted_results: The earliest instruction exception is reported with exactly the preceding instruction prefix committed

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Addition and subtraction (add, sub) are supported

operation.selector: add, subtract, add-one, subtract-one, immediate, shifted-add, and 32-bit-pointer forms
operation.encoding: all currently admitted A1, A3, A4, and A5 scalar arithmetic opcodes

legal_inputs: Zero, one, signed limits, unsigned limits, carry/borrow wrap, immediate extremes, and shift counts 1 through 4

privilege.rule: Application integer arithmetic is independent of privilege

state_read: One or two source GR values and encoded immediate where applicable
state_written: Destination GR and destination NaT bit

predication.rule: A false qualifying predicate suppresses destination value and NaT writes

rows.nat_behavior: Any enabled source NaT propagates to the destination
rows.committed_prefix: The complete arithmetic result and NaT state commit as one GR update
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal register operands
permitted_results: The exact modulo-64 or architected 32-bit-pointer result for the selected form

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: and, andcm, or, xor, signed immediates, addl construction, and movl
operation.encoding: all currently admitted register/immediate logical and large-constant forms

legal_inputs: Complementary and overlapping bit patterns, signed immediate boundaries, and full-width movl constants

privilege.rule: Logical and constant construction instructions are independent of privilege

state_read: Source GRs and encoded immediate fields

predication.rule: False qualification suppresses the destination write

rows.nat_behavior: Source-based forms propagate NaT; source-free constant construction clears destination NaT
rows.committed_prefix: The selected logical or constant value commits once
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal forms
permitted_results: Exact bitwise or sign-extended constant results

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: mpy4 and mpyshl4
operation.encoding: currently admitted I-unit GR multiply encodings

legal_inputs: Zero, one, maximum low halves, and maximum high-half/low-half products

privilege.rule: GR integer multiplication is independent of privilege

state_read: Selected 32-bit halves of two source GRs

predication.rule: False qualification suppresses multiplication and destination writes

rows.nat_behavior: Either source NaT propagates to the destination
rows.committed_prefix: The multiply result commits as one GR update
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal forms
permitted_results: Exact unsigned 32x32 product or exact shifted high/low partial product

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: xma.l, xma.h, xma.hu, and xmpy.hu
operation.encoding: currently admitted F-unit fixed-point multiply-add forms

legal_inputs: Signed and unsigned 64-bit significands with zero-extended addends

privilege.rule: XMA computation is independent of privilege subject to FR enable state

state_read: Two multiplicand significands, one addend significand, and source NaTVal state

predication.rule: False qualification suppresses FR checks and writes

rows.nat_behavior: NaTVal inputs follow floating-register consumption rules
fault_priority: A false qualifier suppresses disabled-register checks; an enabled XMA reports the first disabled FR operand
rows.committed_prefix: The selected low or high half commits once as a canonical integer
rows.restart_rule: A fault restarts at the XMA instruction without a destination write
permitted_results: The selected low or signed/unsigned high 64 bits of the exact 128-bit multiply-add

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Four classes of instructions are defined

operation.selector: logical/arithmetic shifts, shrp, dep, dep.z, extr, extr.u, sxt, and zxt
operation.encoding: all currently admitted I-unit shift, bitfield, and extension forms

legal_inputs: Counts zero through and beyond 64, field boundaries, signed fields, and 8/16/32-bit extension boundaries

privilege.rule: Shift and bitfield instructions are independent of privilege

state_read: Source GRs, count GR, and position/length immediates

predication.rule: False qualification suppresses all result effects

rows.nat_behavior: Any enabled source or count NaT propagates to the destination
rows.committed_prefix: The full shifted, extracted, deposited, or extended result commits once
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal fields
permitted_results: Exact logical, sign-filled, funnel, masked, merged, sign-extended, or zero-extended result

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: popcnt, clz, mux1, mux2, and all implemented czx scans
operation.encoding: currently admitted bit-count and byte/halfword arrangement/search forms

legal_inputs: Zero, all-one, alternating-bit, lane-unique, and every zero-index placement

privilege.rule: Character and bit-string operations are independent of privilege

state_read: Source GR bit or lane image and mux selector
state_written: Count, index, or arranged destination GR

predication.rule: False qualification suppresses the destination write

rows.nat_behavior: Source NaT propagates to the destination
rows.committed_prefix: The count, index, or arrangement result commits once
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal forms
permitted_results: Exact population/leading-zero count, zero index, or selector-defined byte/halfword arrangement

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 3: Instruction Set Reference

operation.selector: predicate, NaT, alias, legal slot, reserved field, and precise fault behavior
operation.encoding: every admitted scalar form plus an invalid POPCNT reserved source field

legal_inputs: True and false qualifiers, source/destination aliases, source NaT, every legal unit placement, and enabled/nullified reserved fields

privilege.rule: Application scalar legality is independent of privilege

state_read: Entry predicate and GR/NaT images, encoding fields, and slot type
state_written: Legal destination or architectural interruption state

predication.rule: A false qualifier suppresses legal writes and reserved-if-qp-one faults

rows.nat_behavior: Source forms propagate NaT, source-free forms clear it, and nullified forms preserve destination NaT
fault_priority: Predicate qualification precedes reserved-if-qp-one checks; enabled reserved fields fault before destination or suffix effects
rows.committed_prefix: Legal writes commit once; an enabled reserved encoding preserves its prefix and suppresses its target and suffix
rows.restart_rule: Illegal Operation saves the exact faulting bundle and slot
permitted_results: Exact legal result/NaT, no effect when nullified, or precise Illegal Operation for the enabled reserved field

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: the exact current 40-opcode scalar admission set
operation.encoding: two deterministic generated base/boundary rows per admitted opcode

legal_inputs: Declaratively generated ordinary, zero, limit, overflow, excessive-count, and field-boundary inputs

privilege.rule: Generated application-integer cases are independent of privilege

state_read: Literal test-owned operand and encoding fields

predication.rule: The model separately validates false-qualifier non-effects

rows.nat_behavior: The state matrix independently validates source-count-based NaT propagation for all 40 rows
rows.committed_prefix: Every generated legal operation commits its one modeled result once
rows.restart_rule: Not exercised by legal generated rows
permitted_results: Every generated guest result equals the independently calculated Python integer model

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture

operation.selector: store to an instruction image followed by fc.i, sync.i, srlz.i, and re-entry
operation.encoding: legal store and cache/serialization instructions operating on an aligned bundle image

legal_inputs: A valid replacement instruction image written through an architecturally legal data store
The affected cache line is flushed and instruction visibility is synchronized and serialized before re-entry

privilege.rule: The sequence applies at every privilege level where the mapping permits the store and cache-control operations

state_written: architectural results of the replacement instruction

predication.rule: A false-predicated store or maintenance instruction performs no corresponding state update

rows.nat_behavior: A NaT-consuming address or store operand follows the selected instruction's fault semantics and cannot make the replacement executable
rows.committed_prefix: The data store, cache-line flush, instruction synchronization, and serialization complete in program order before re-entry
rows.restart_rule: If a separately defined interruption occurs, retry follows that instruction's architectural restart rule; successful re-entry fetches the coherent replacement
permitted_results: After the complete synchronization sequence, re-entry executes the replacement instruction image and not stale translated code

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: read of the memory and ALAT state occurred after the update

operation.selector: ordinary RAM load followed by a dependent instruction
operation.encoding: legal load and dependency-correct consumer spanning either one translator TB or an internal TB boundary

legal_inputs: A naturally aligned mapped RAM operand
A legal consumer of the successful load result

privilege.rule: The rule applies at every privilege level for which the mapping grants read access

predication.rule: A false-predicated load performs no memory read and produces no destination for a later consumer

rows.nat_behavior: A successful ordinary load clears the destination NaT indication; a NaT address follows the separately catalogued fault rule
rows.committed_prefix: The successful load result is available to the dependency-correct consumer independent of an implementation-created TB boundary
rows.restart_rule: Not exercised for the valid mapped RAM input
permitted_results: The load and consumer produce identical architectural values with and without a translator-created continuation boundary

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: The update is done after the load or store operation

operation.selector: successful post-increment store followed by same-group and later memory consumers
operation.encoding: legal immediate base-update store at a final-bundle memory safepoint

legal_inputs: A mapped naturally aligned destination
A legal immediate update and dependency-correct same-group consumers

privilege.rule: The rule applies at every privilege level for which the mapping grants write access

predication.rule: A false-predicated update-form store performs neither the memory write nor the base update

rows.nat_behavior: A successful store preserves unrelated NaT state; a NaT-consuming operand follows the separately catalogued fault rule
rows.committed_prefix: The memory write completes and the base is updated exactly once before the final architectural state is observed
rows.restart_rule: Not exercised for the valid mapped destination; a faulting variant belongs to MEM-012
permitted_results: The stored value, one post-increment, immutable same-group entry-base read, and successor results match across resident and split execution

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: At the point of exception delivery for a correctly formed instruction group

operation.selector: faulting update-form load after a multi-bundle successful prefix
operation.encoding: legal slot-1 load whose NaT address raises Register NaT Consumption

legal_inputs: A dependency-correct issue group with successful GR, BR, PR, store, and base-update prefixes
A NaT-tagged load address in slot 1

privilege.rule: The commitment rule applies at every privilege level where the instructions are legal

state_written: architectural effects of instructions before the fault

predication.rule: The tested load is enabled; a false-predicated load would not consume the address NaT or fault

rows.nat_behavior: The enabled load consumes a NaT address and faults without changing its destination, base, or NaT state
fault_priority: The enabled slot-1 NaT-consuming load is the earliest faulting instruction and is reported after every prior instruction update
rows.committed_prefix: Every successful store/base, BR, PR, and GR prefix effect is visible exactly once; the faulting load and suffix are suppressed
rows.restart_rule: Repair would retry at the saved slot-1 load without replaying the already committed prefix

operation.selector: AR64 pfm, pec, and ppl
operation.encoding: I-unit register-form AR write followed by serialized AR read

legal_inputs: Every field singleton under the required PFM baseline
Empty and maximum legal PFM/pec/ppl images

privilege.rule: PFS is application-visible; the complete field matrix executes at CPL0

state_read: exact interruption vector, ISR, and I-slot EI for field faults

predication.rule: False qualification suppresses reserved-field validation and leaves PFS unchanged

rows.nat_behavior: A register-form NaT source raises Register NaT Consumption before reserved-field validation
rows.committed_prefix: Each stopped setup prefix commits; a faulting requested PFS value does not
rows.restart_rule: With PSR.ic clear, the handler validates ISR.EI and branches to the test-owned continuation
permitted_results: All 25 literal or dependent reserved values fault exactly; all 48 legal values preserve exactly

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: AR16 mode, pl, be, and loadrs
operation.encoding: M-unit register-form AR write followed by serialized AR read

legal_inputs: Every defined singleton bit at CPL0
CPL3 request for RSC.pl=0
False-qualified and NaT-source reserved values

rows.nat_behavior: A register-form NaT source raises Register NaT Consumption before reserved-field validation
rows.committed_prefix: Each stopped setup prefix commits; a faulting requested RSC value does not
rows.restart_rule: With PSR.ic clear, the handler validates ISR and branches to the test-owned continuation

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture
specification.anchor: Writing BSPSTORE (mov to BSPSTORE application register) has side-effects on all three

operation.selector: AR18 BSPSTORE, AR17 BSP, and internal RSE.BspLoad
operation.encoding: M-unit register-form BSPSTORE writes followed by architectural pointer reads

legal_inputs: All 64 BSPSTORE address offsets selected by bits 8:3
Every ignored low-bit pattern
Empty complete dirty partition
True and false qualification
Same-group and post-stop reads

privilege.rule: BSPSTORE is application-visible when RSC.mode is zero; the complete matrix executes at CPL0

state_read: RSE.BspLoad through the final system snapshot

predication.rule: False qualification leaves all three RSE pointers unchanged

rows.nat_behavior: A qualified BSPSTORE write makes RNAT contents undefined; no post-write RNAT value is constrained
rows.committed_prefix: Each qualified rebase publishes the aligned BSPSTORE/BspLoad and dirty-partition-derived BSP at the issue-group stop; same-group reads use the entry image
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for complete-frame, implemented-address inputs
permitted_results: For zero dirty words, BSP equals aligned BSPSTORE except at address offset 63 where BSP skips one intervening RNAT collection word; false qualification is inert and same-group reads see the old image

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: RSE Backing Store Pointer for Memory Stores

operation.selector: AR18 pointer and AR19 NaT collection
operation.encoding: M-unit register-form writes followed by serialized reads

legal_inputs: Every BSPSTORE pointer bit 63:3
Every ignored low-bit combination
Every RNAT bit 0:63

privilege.rule: The field matrix executes at CPL0 with RSC.mode=0; access is application-visible when mode is zero

state_read: exact normalized BSPSTORE and RNAT readback
exact exception state for the RSC.mode priority probe
state_written: BSPSTORE pointer and architecturally associated BSP/RNAT state

predication.rule: False qualification leaves both registers unchanged as established by the complete selector matrix

rows.nat_behavior: Nonzero RSC.mode raises Illegal Operation before a register-form RNAT source NaT is consumed
rows.committed_prefix: Every legal write/read pair is stopped; the RSC.mode-illegal RNAT write does not commit
rows.restart_rule: The priority probe stops at the exact General Exception vector and saved IIP
permitted_results: BSPSTORE bits 63:3 and RNAT bits 62:0 preserve; BSPSTORE bits 2:0 and RNAT bit63 read zero; nonzero RSC.mode blocks RNAT before NaT

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: The cmpxchg target is loaded with the zero-extended contents

operation.selector: AR32 and cmpxchg1, cmpxchg2, cmpxchg4, cmpxchg8
operation.encoding: acquire and release compare-exchange forms at every width

legal_inputs: Naturally aligned mapped operands
Matching and mismatching CCV values
True and false qualification

privilege.rule: CCV and compare-exchange are application-visible; the complete effect matrix executes at CPL0

state_written: selected low-width memory value only when qualified and equal

predication.rule: False qualification changes neither destination nor memory

rows.nat_behavior: A qualified NaT operand is governed by the compare-exchange instruction's Register NaT Consumption rules; valid ordinary operands are selected here
rows.committed_prefix: A qualified comparison returns the old value and atomically commits either the selected-width replacement or no memory change
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for the selected valid mapped operands
permitted_results: The destination is the zero-extended old value; memory changes exactly when old value equals CCV; false qualification preserves both

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: When a br.call or brl.call is executed, the CFM, EC, and PSR.cpl are copied to the PFS

operation.selector: PFS, CFM, EC, CPL, link BR, and RSE frame state
operation.encoding: br.call immediate/indirect and brl.call across B3, B5, and X4 forms

legal_inputs: Complete caller frames
Aligned direct, long, and indirect targets
True, false, nullified, and first-taken-suppressed calls
Split and combined TB execution

privilege.rule: A taken call captures current PSR.cpl in PFS.ppl without changing live CPL

state_written: callee CFM and RSE frame mapping

predication.rule: A false, nullified, or first-taken-suppressed call changes none of PFS, link, CFM, or RSE state

rows.nat_behavior: Caller output NaT state remaps with its value into the callee-visible frame
rows.committed_prefix: A selected call atomically publishes link, packed PFS, callee CFM, and RSE remapping as its control-flow transition
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for complete-frame aligned-target inputs
permitted_results: PFS is exactly old CFM/EC/CPL, link is bundle+16, the callee frame preserves output value/NaT identity, live EC/CPL remain unchanged, and false or suppressed calls are inert

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: When a br.ret is executed, the PFS is copied to the CFM and EC

operation.selector: PFS, CFM, EC, CPL, dynamic BR target, and RSE frame state
operation.encoding: B4 br.ret with clean and incomplete frames

legal_inputs: Valid PFS caller-frame images
Aligned dynamic targets
True and false qualification
Clean and four-word incomplete SOL4 frames
Mapped and faulting backing-store words around an RNAT slot

privilege.rule: PFS.ppl is copied to PSR.cpl unless that would increase privilege; Lower-Privilege Transfer precedes a Taken Branch trap

predication.rule: A false-qualified return preserves CFM, EC, CPL, and RSE state

rows.nat_behavior: Mandatory RSE fills restore register NaTs from the architecturally selected RNAT collection and retain exact retry prefix semantics
rows.committed_prefix: A taken return restores PFS-derived state before post-target traps; a mandatory-fill fault preserves its completed prefix and retries the faulting word once through RFI
rows.restart_rule: An IFS.v=0 RFI resumes the post-return target and completes the faulted fill, including after a fresh-process save/load checkpoint
permitted_results: Valid returns restore exact CFM/EC/CPL and aligned control flow; clean and RNAT-crossing incomplete frames restore exact values/NaTs; faults report exact target/source state and retry idempotently

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture

operation.selector: AR32 CCV, AR36 UNAT, AR65 LC, and AR66 EC
operation.encoding: unit-correct register-form writes followed by serialized reads

legal_inputs: Every singleton bit of each named register

privilege.rule: All four registers are application-visible; the complete matrix executes at CPL0

state_read: exact normalized CCV, UNAT, LC, and EC readback

predication.rule: False-qualified accesses are inert as established by the complete selector matrix

rows.nat_behavior: A register-form NaT source raises Register NaT Consumption before the write
rows.committed_prefix: Each explicit write and readback is isolated by a stop
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal singleton inputs; the operation-wide NaT probe stops at its architected vector
permitted_results: All bits of CCV, UNAT, and LC preserve; EC bits 5:0 preserve and bits 63:6 read zero

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: The ld8.fill and st8.spill instructions implicitly access

operation.selector: AR36 bit selected by spill/fill address bits 8:3
operation.encoding: paired st8.spill and ld8.fill across all 64 selectors

legal_inputs: All 64 naturally aligned mapped address selectors
NaT and ordinary spill sources
True and false qualification
Distinct selected bits in one instruction group

privilege.rule: UNAT spill/fill behavior is application-visible; the complete effect matrix executes at CPL0

state_written: selected UNAT bit and spill word
fill destination GR value and NaT

predication.rule: False-qualified spill and fill preserve UNAT, memory, destination value, and destination NaT

rows.nat_behavior: st8.spill records the source NaT in the address-selected UNAT bit; ld8.fill restores that bit into the destination NaT
rows.committed_prefix: Each legal distinct-bit spill update commits its payload and selected UNAT bit; each subsequent fill observes the completed image
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for the selected valid mapped operands
permitted_results: Address bits 8:3 select exactly one UNAT bit; all 64 payload and NaT values round-trip and false qualification is inert

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture

operation.selector: banked GR16-GR31 values and NaT bits
operation.encoding: precise interruption entry, RFI with IPSR.bn=1, and explicit BSW.0

legal_inputs: Distinct values in every GR16-GR31 of both banks
A bank-0 NaT at r16 and a bank-1 NaT at r31
An enabled precise illegal-operation fault while bank 1 is selected

privilege.rule: Interruption delivery, IPSR restoration, RFI, and explicit bank-switch instructions execute in the system environment

state_read: both banked GR16-GR31 value and NaT images

predication.rule: The deliberately faulting instruction is predicated true initially and its predicate is cleared by the handler before restart

rows.nat_behavior: Each bank preserves its independent NaT image across interruption entry, RFI, and explicit BSW.0
fault_priority: The illegal-operation fault is the only enabled interruption source
rows.committed_prefix: Slot 0 commits before the precise slot-1 fault; slot 2 commits only after RFI resumes with the faulting predicate false
rows.restart_rule: Interruption entry forces bank 0, while RFI restores IPSR.bn=1 and resumes the interrupted bundle
permitted_results: The handler sees the exact bank-0 image with PSR.bn=0 and IPSR.bn=1; resumed code sees the exact bank-1 image; BSW.0 restores the unchanged bank-0 image

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Registers are rotated towards larger register numbers

operation.selector: complete f32-f127 rotating region
operation.encoding: ninety-six qualifying WTOP operations and GETF.SIG observation at every RRB.FR base

legal_inputs: Unique canonical integer significands 1 through 96 in f32-f127 with low and high FR access enabled

privilege.rule: Modulo-scheduled floating-register renaming is application-visible when FR access is enabled

state_written: CFM.rrb.fr and the logical f32-f127 view

predication.rule: Each WTOP is qualified true; observation transfers use p0

rows.nat_behavior: Canonical integer significands are not NaTVal
fault_priority: PSR.dfl and PSR.dfh are cleared before FR initialization and observation
rows.committed_prefix: Each stopped WTOP and each stopped GETF.SIG comparison commits independently
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for enabled legal operands
permitted_results: The full one-step f32-f127 permutation and every subsequent endpoint observation follow modulo-96 renaming; the complete file and RRB.FR return after 96 rotations

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Registers are rotated towards larger register numbers

operation.selector: GR32 through GR(31+SOR) for every legal SOR from 8 through 96
operation.encoding: ALLOC with each legal SOR followed by one qualifying WTOP

legal_inputs: Distinct values in every member of each selected rotating region
A NaT on the lowest physical rotating GR
A 96-register current frame

privilege.rule: Modulo-scheduled stacked-GR renaming is application-visible for legal current-frame operands

state_read: selected physical rotating GR values and NaT bits
state_written: CFM.rrb.gr and the logical rotating-GR value/NaT view

predication.rule: The single WTOP is qualified true; observations use p0 and mismatch predicates

rows.nat_behavior: The NaT bit rotates with its GR value from logical r32 to logical r33
rows.committed_prefix: Each stopped initialization, WTOP, and comparison commits independently
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal current-frame operands
permitted_results: For all twelve legal SOR sizes, every logical rotating GR advances one position with wraparound, its NaT follows it, and CFM contains the exact modulo-SOR RRB.GR

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 1: Application Architecture
specification.anchor: Registers are rotated towards larger register numbers

operation.selector: complete p16-p63 rotating region including WTOP p63 update
operation.encoding: forty-eight qualifying WTOP operations for each of six independent predicate codewords

legal_inputs: Six bit-index codewords that uniquely distinguish every p16-p63 position

privilege.rule: Modulo-scheduled predicate renaming and application loop branches are available at all privilege levels

state_written: old logical p63 before each WTOP rotation

predication.rule: Each WTOP is qualified true; WTOP writes false to old logical p63 before rotating that value into new logical p16

rows.nat_behavior: Predicate registers do not carry NaT state
rows.committed_prefix: Each stopped predicate transfer, WTOP, and comparison commits independently
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for legal operands

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 2: System Architecture
specification.anchor: the BSP pointer is set to the address presented plus the size of the dirty partition plus the size of any intervening NaT collections

operation.selector: dirty sizes 0 through 96 and BSPSTORE address slots 0 through 63
operation.encoding: real alloc/call dirty growth followed by stopped mov to BSPSTORE and BSP observation

legal_inputs: Every dirty-partition size from zero through 96
Every BSPSTORE RNAT-relative offset from zero through 63
Complete frames and implemented mapped addresses

privilege.rule: The complete RSE pointer matrix executes in the system guest with RSC.mode zero

state_read: presented BSPSTORE address and RNAT-relative offset

predication.rule: The complete dirty matrix uses p0; false qualification and same-group visibility are established by REG-008-BSPSTORE-BSP-REBASE-EFFECT

rows.nat_behavior: The BSP address skips one RNAT collection word after each 63 backing-store register slots; the post-write RNAT value is undefined
rows.committed_prefix: Each stopped call grows one dirty register and each stopped BSPSTORE write atomically rebases all three RSE pointers
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for complete frames and implemented mapped addresses
permitted_results: For each of 6,208 cases BSP equals aligned BSPSTORE plus eight times the dirty count plus eight times floor((offset + dirty) / 63)

specification.title: Intel Itanium Architecture Software Developer's Manual, Volume 3: Instruction Set Reference
specification.anchor: After this instruction completes execution BSPSTORE is equal to BSP

operation.selector: 96 dirty stacked registers spanning completed and partial RNAT collections
operation.encoding: legal slot-zero flushrs first in its instruction group

legal_inputs: A complete 96-register dirty partition
Distinct values one through 96 with alternating NaTs
A mapped writable backing store starting at RNAT-relative offset 63

privilege.rule: The mandatory RSE flush executes at CPL0 with RSC.mode zero

predication.rule: FLUSHRS is architecturally unpredicated and must be first in its instruction group

rows.nat_behavior: Dirty register NaTs are written into the address-selected RNAT collection; a completed collection is stored and the final partial collection remains in AR.RNAT
rows.committed_prefix: Mandatory RSE stores commit in backing-store order; successful completion empties the dirty partition and sets BSPSTORE equal to BSP
rows.restart_rule: No restart occurs for the selected valid mapped writable backing store
permitted_results: All 96 payloads and NaTs occupy their exact register/RNAT positions, BSPSTORE equals BSP, and BspLoad is either preserved at the original BSPSTORE or advanced to BSP
